zh.Pitminster, Taunton Deane, Somerset, Eng: 英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特
zh.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV於1507年出生在英國英格蘭薩默塞特。他於1525年與Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton在英國英格蘭薩默塞特結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1529年在英國英格蘭薩默塞特比特明斯特享年22歲。

zh-hans.Pitminster, Taunton Deane, Somerset, Eng: 英国英格兰薩默塞特比特明斯特
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV于1507年出生在英国英格兰薩默塞特。他于1525年与Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton在英国英格兰薩默塞特结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1529年在英国英格兰薩默塞特比特明斯特享年22岁。

nameMeanings.name: Brixton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish: habitational name perhaps from a lost or unidentified place, although possibly brought to Scotland from one or more of the English Brintons under 2 below.

English: habitational name from Brinton in Norfolk, named in Old English as Brȳningtūn ‘settlement (Old English tūn) associated with (-ing-) Brȳni’ (a personal name based on Old English bryne ‘fire, flame’), or from any of various other places with names of the same origin, such as Brineton in Staffordshire, Great Brington in Essex, Brimpton in Berkshire, Brimington in Derbyshire, Brenton in Devon, Brington in Cambridgeshire or (Great and Little) Brington in Northamptonshire.
History: William Brinton (1635–99) came from Staffordshire, England, to West Chester, PA, in 1684–85.

variants.name: Garnto
meanings: Presumably an altered form of English Brinton , via the intermediate forms Brinto and Grinto. Compare Gornto .

variants.name: Gornto
meanings: Presumably an altered form of English Brinton , via the intermediate forms Brinto and Grinto. Compare Garnto .
History: Edward Brinto, alias Gornto, is listed as one of the original settlers (a mason) in Jamestown VA in 1607.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: SEVENOAK
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English: locative name from Sevenoaks (Kent).

variants.name: Swinnock
meanings: English:  see  Sevenoaks  .

variants.name: Senneck
meanings: English: a Dorset variant of   Sevenoaks  , from the old pronunciation of the place-name. There is no evidence to connect it with Savernake (Wilts).

variants.name: Sunnucks
meanings: English:  see  Sevenoaks  .

variants.name: Sonnex
meanings: English:  see  Sevenoaks  .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Cambodian: written អ៊ីវ, unexplained. — Note: In the population figure published by the US Census Bureau, the Roman number IV (meaning ‘the fourth’ of the four bearers of the name) may also be counted as a surname Iv.

nameMeanings.name: Newman
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English newe ‘new’ (i.e. newly arrived or newly appointed) + man ‘man’ (Old English nīwe + mann).

Americanized form (translation into English) of surnames meaning ‘new man, newcomer’, for example German and Jewish (Ashkenazic) Neumann , Swedish Nyman , Polish Nowak , Czech or Slovak Novák, Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Novak .

variants.name: Neumann
meanings: Gaelic equivalent of Edmund .

variants.name: Numan
meanings: ‘well-disposed, cheerful’; also ‘wise’.

variants.name: Xenos
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘mankind’ in Hebrew), borne by a son of Seth and grandson of Adam (Genesis 4:26), who allegedly lived for 905 years.

variants.name: Snowman
meanings: English: perhaps a variant of Nowman, itself a variant of Newman , + a prosthetic S-. For the unetymological S- compare Starbuck .
Americanized form (translation into English) of Jewish (from Poland) Sznejman or Sznajman: artificial name composed of Yiddish shney ‘snow’ + man ‘man’.

Americanized form (translation into English) of German Schneemann: habitational name for a man from Schneen in Göttingen or from Schnee near Dortmund and Stade, translated into English as though it were from German Schnee ‘snow’ + Mann ‘man’.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV was born in 1507, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. He married Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton in 1525, in Somerset, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1529, in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 22.

fr.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV est né en 1507, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il a épousé Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton en 1525, à Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1529, à Pitminster, Somerset, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 22 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV wurde 1507 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom geboren. Er hat 1525 in Somerset, England, United Kingdom Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1529 im Alter von 22 Jahren in Pitminster, Somerset, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV è nato il nel 1507 nel Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì nel 1529, nel Pitminster, Somerset, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 22 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Newman John Brixton of SEVENOAK IV(은)는 1507년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 태어났다. 그는 1525년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트에서 Lady Joanna Alwyn Gaylord Brixton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1529년 영국 잉글랜드 서머세트 피트민스터에서 22세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он умер в 1529 г. в местности питминстир, сомирсет, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 22 лет.
